title: Why Scarsdale Homeowners Choose a Design-Build Firm for Their Next Project
excerpt: When you work with a design-build firm in Scarsdale, you're working with one person who handles both the architecture and construction. That means fewer miscommunications and a project that actually matches your vision.
content:
Scarsdale homeowners have options when it comes to renovations and additions. You can hire an architect, then hunt for a general contractor. You can work with a builder who has connections to designers. Or you can go straight to a design-build firm where the same licensed professional handles both sides of the work.

There's a real difference in how projects run depending on which path you choose.

What Makes Design-Build Different

A design-build firm brings the architect and contractor together under one roof. In Scarsdale, that means when you work with House Architecture & Construction, you're working with Silvio M. Luca—a licensed architect in New York and a licensed general contractor. He's the one who listens to your ideas, draws up the plans, and then oversees the construction himself.

This matters because the person designing your space is the same person who has to build it. There's accountability. There's no finger-pointing between architects and contractors about what was promised versus what's possible. The vision stays intact from sketch to finished room.

Scarsdale Homes Need Someone Who Understands Them

Scarsdale isn't like everywhere else. The homes here have character. Some are colonial estates with details worth preserving. Others are mid-century designs that deserve thoughtful updates. A few are newer builds where families want to add a primary suite or expand the kitchen.

When you work with a residential architect in Scarsdale who's also your general contractor, that person knows the building department, the permit process, and what the neighbors' homes look like. They understand the neighborhood and won't push you toward something that doesn't fit.

Like most towns in Westchester, Scarsdale has its own building department and specific permit requirements. Working with someone based right here in Eastchester who serves Scarsdale regularly means the paperwork moves smoothly and approvals happen on time.

What You Can Actually Do

Home additions in Westchester are some of the most popular projects because they add square footage without moving. A second story, a primary suite, an expanded kitchen—these are the kinds of projects that transform daily life. Historic restorations matter too, especially in a town where older homes have real value.

You might want an open-concept conversion to match how families live today. Or a full kitchen remodel where every detail is exactly right. A bathroom renovation that feels like a spa. Custom new construction on a lot you own. A general contractor in Westchester who's also a licensed architect can design all of it and build all of it.

One Person, Start to Finish

When Silvio runs your project personally, he's there from the initial conversation through final walkthrough. You're not managing relationships between two separate vendors with different schedules and priorities.

If you're thinking about a renovation or addition for your Scarsdale home, call (914) 224-7383 for a free consultation. Let's talk about what you want and how to make it happen.

Curious what this could look like for your home? Take a look at our Scarsdale design-build firm page, or call (914) 224-7383 to set up a free consultation.
